name: Dongxiang

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Dongxiang (dong1285).

notes: Skeleton. Dongxiang (Santa) is a Mongolic language of Gansu, China, here written in an experimental Latin (Pinyin-based) romanization. Initials follow Pinyin conventions: unaspirated stops spelled b d g (p t k), aspirates p t k, the sibilant/affricate series j q x (tɕ tɕʰ ɕ) and z c s (ts tsʰ s), the retroflex digraphs zh ch sh, and r. Vowels a o e i u ü take their Pinyin values. Because this romanization was never fully standardized and Dongxiang phonology diverges from Mandarin, several consonant values (notably the retroflex series and uvular reflexes) are approximate; tone/register and vowel length are not written and are left unencoded, so a raised PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Dongxiang (dong1285)
